task_text: Look at the sprint 14 assignments and testing log, then tell me which tasks are at risk of missing the sprint deadline. Write your assessment to sprint14_deadline_risk.txt.

---------|-------------------------------------------|-------------------|--------|------------|------------

Project Helios — QA Testing Log: Sprint 14
QA Engineer: Kenji Watanabe
Period: 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-11 (ongoing)

=== Test Suite: Authentication Flows ===
TC-401  OAuth token issuance              PASS

Suite result: 4/4 PASS

=== Test Suite: Data Pipeline — Batch Ingestion ===
TC-501  CSV ingestion (10k records)                  PASS
TC-502  JSON ingestion (10k records)                 PASS
TC-503  XML ingestion (5k records)                   PASS

Suite result: 1/4 complete

=== Open Defects ===
HEL-D-014  Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input format.
           Affects ~40% of Meridian source data. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Blocked pending Meridian sample data (due 2026-07-15).

HEL-D-015  Large file ingestion (>200k records) causes memory overflow.
           Pipeline crashes at approximately 210k record mark. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Root cause under investigation.

Overall sprint 14 test status: 10/15 test cases executed, 2 FAIL (open), 3 not yet run.

input.content:
Sprint 14 Deadline Risk Assessment
Sprint window: 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-18
Based on: sprint_14_task_assignments.txt and helios_testing_log_sprint14.txt
Assessment date reference: testing log through 2026-07-11 (mid-sprint)

Tasks at risk of missing the sprint deadline
============================================

1. HEL-243 — Data ingestion: date format normalization (Amir Hassan)
   - Status: Blocked | Due: 2026-07-15 | Points: 5
   - Open defect HEL-D-014 (HIGH): date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY; blocked pending Meridian sample data (due 2026-07-15).
   - Open defect HEL-D-015 (HIGH): large-file ingestion memory overflow also owned by Amir; root cause under investigation.
   - Risk: High — blocked with dependency landing on the task due date; two open high-severity defects.

2. HEL-246 — UAT environment: server provisioning (Nadia Petrov)
   - Status: At Risk | Due: 2026-07-16 | Points: 8
   - Explicitly flagged At Risk in the assignment board; large 8-point item with due date before sprint end.
   - Risk: High — already marked At Risk with limited calendar buffer.

3. HEL-247 — Performance benchmark: dashboard load (Derek Okafor)
   - Status: Not Started | Due: 2026-07-18 | Points: 3
   - Dependent test TC-604 (load time benchmark) has not been run; dashboard suite only 1/4 complete.
   - Risk: Medium–High — work not started with due date on the final day of the sprint.

4. HEL-242 — Dashboard: chart rendering module (Tomas Rivera)
   - Status: In Progress | Due: 2026-07-16 | Points: 8
   - Related tests: TC-602 (line chart) still In Progress; TC-603 (bar chart) Not Run.
   - Risk: Medium — 8-point item due mid/late sprint with incomplete verification.

5. HEL-241 — Dashboard: data table component (Fatima Al-Rashid)
   - Status: In Progress | Due: 2026-07-14 | Points: 5
   - TC-601 passed, but task still In Progress with due date already close relative to the log period (through 2026-07-11).
   - Risk: Medium — due soon and not yet Done.

6. HEL-248 — GeoSync license renewal coordination (Raj Patel)
   - Status: In Progress | Due: 2026-07-14 | Points: 2
   - Still In Progress with due date near/at the early-week cutoff relative to current log progress.
   - Risk: Medium — small scope but not closed before/on due date.

Tasks not currently assessed as deadline risks
==============================================
- HEL-244 — Integration test suite: auth flows: Done; related suite 4/4 PASS.
- HEL-245 — Integration test suite: data pipeline: In Progress, due 2026-07-18; pipeline suite partially failing due to HEL-D-014/015 (owned by Amir), but Kenji’s suite work itself has runway to sprint end. Monitor for knock-on delay if defects remain open.

Summary
=======
Highest-priority risks: HEL-243 (blocked + open high-severity defects), HEL-246 (explicit At Risk), and HEL-247 (not started). Secondary watch items: HEL-242, HEL-241, and HEL-248 due to mid-sprint status vs. their due dates.

content.text:
Assessment written to `sprint14_deadline_risk.txt`.

**Highest risk of missing the sprint deadline:**
- **HEL-243** (Amir) — Blocked; open high-severity defects HEL-D-014 / HEL-D-015; sample-data dependency due same day as the task
- **HEL-246** (Nadia) — Explicitly marked At Risk; 8 points due 2026-07-16
- **HEL-247** (Derek) — Not Started; due last day of sprint; related load benchmark not run

**Secondary watch items:** HEL-242, HEL-241, HEL-248 (still In Progress against near/mid-sprint due dates)
